frame_analyses.description: Needle is being prepared for placement.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle held properly and sutures ready.

frame_analyses.description: Needle is being passed through the marked point on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper positioning of needle at the marked point.

frame_analyses.description: First throw of surgeon's knot being initiated.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Using right hand primarily, proper tension applied.

frame_analyses.description: First throw completed, with proper tension on the knot.
Second throw being attempted with a hand switch.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Successful hand switch, single throw initiated.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw being tied after switching hands again.
Cutting of the suture ends in progress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both ends are being cut inside the trainer box.

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot tied with appropriate tension.
Third throw finalized with good tension.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: The drain slit was closed effectively with no visible gaps.

technique_summary: The trainee successfully completed the intracorporeal suturing task with minor deviations. All knots were tied securely, and hand switches were executed correctly.
improvement_suggestions: Work on reducing suture deviation from marks.
Increase speed while maintaining security and precision.
strengths: Effective hand switches between suturing throws.
